elements.text: iDoc is a browser-based prototype for interactive documents. This standalone HTML is the generated reader. Authoring now happens in the Flutter desktop app, which exports the embedded JSON into this runtime.

elements.title: Recommended workflow
elements.text: Use iDoc Studio on Windows to create pages, blocks, and actions. Then export a fresh .idoc.html file for sharing.
Recommended save name for the standalone file: example.idoc.html
Reader controls stay in the runtime
The generated HTML keeps navigation, theme switching, search, links, popups, and question logic. Editing controls are intentionally removed from the runtime.
Author in the app. Read in the file.

items: The HTML file keeps only the interactive reading runtime.

elements.label: Open MDN in a new tab

elements.text: The runtime still supports questions, answer reveal, and JSON export. Search terms like export, runtime, and Flutter should match here.

elements.prompt: Where should users author new iDoc documents now?
options: Directly inside the exported HTML runtime.
Inside the Flutter Windows authoring app.
elements.explanation: The Flutter editor is now the source of truth. The HTML file is the generated runtime output.

elements.title: Shareable output
elements.text: You can send the exported .idoc.html file to someone else and they can open it directly in the browser.

open-intro-popup.title: About this demo
open-intro-popup.content: This popup is defined in the document action map. The generated HTML file stays transparent and self-contained.
